evidence: 310,249 cleaned sessions added 3–20 items to cart but did not purchase
hypothesis: Showing checkout reassurance or free-shipping messaging will increase cart-to-purchase conversion.
variant: Cart/checkout page with reassurance or free-shipping message
decision_rule: Roll out if conversion uplift is positive and guardrail metrics do not decline materially

evidence: 147,445 sessions viewed 7+ products but did not purchase
hypothesis: Helping users compare and discover relevant products will increase view-to-cart conversion.
variant: Recently viewed or similar-product recommendation module
decision_rule: Roll out if view-to-cart improves without reducing purchase conversion

evidence: 9,215 abandoned cart sessions and 74.25% cart abandonment
hypothesis: Delivery reassurance will reduce hesitation for vacuum products.
control: Current vacuum product and cart pages
variant: Delivery reassurance or free-delivery threshold message
decision_rule: Roll out if cart-to-purchase conversion improves and AOV remains stable

evidence: £50+ products had the lowest view-to-cart rate at 7.82%
hypothesis: Trust, delivery and payment reassurance will increase add-to-cart behaviour for high-priced products.
variant: Product trust badges, returns reassurance or payment reassurance
decision_rule: Roll out if view-to-cart improves without increasing returns

evidence: Under £5 products had 696,183 cart sessions and 82.07% cart abandonment
hypothesis: Users may abandon low-value baskets if delivery cost or checkout effort feels too high relative to basket value.
variant: Free-shipping threshold, basket-building prompt, bundle offer or simplified checkout
decision_rule: Roll out if cart-to-purchase conversion improves without reducing margin per order
